examples.title: Encapsulation Example
examples.description: Demonstrates encapsulation with a Bank Account class
examples.code:
CLASS BankAccount
 PRIVATE: balance
 PUBLIC:
 METHOD deposit(amount)
 IF amount > 0 THEN
 balance = balance + amount
 RETURN TRUE
 END IF
 RETURN FALSE
 END METHOD
 
 METHOD withdraw(amount)
 IF amount > 0 AND amount <= balance THEN
 balance = balance - amount
 RETURN TRUE
 END IF
 RETURN FALSE
 END METHOD
 
 METHOD getBalance()
 RETURN balance
 END METHOD
END CLASS
examples.explanation: The balance is private (hidden), accessed only through public methods deposit(), withdraw(), and getBalance(). This is encapsulation - protecting data from direct modification.

examples.title: Inheritance Example
examples.code:
CLASS Animal
 PUBLIC:
 METHOD speak()
 PRINT "Animal speaks"
 END METHOD
 
 METHOD eat()
 PRINT "Animal eats"
 END METHOD
END CLASS

CLASS Dog EXTENDS Animal
 PUBLIC:
 METHOD speak()
 PRINT "Dog barks"
 END METHOD
END CLASS

// Usage:
Dog d = NEW Dog()
d.speak() // Output: Dog barks
d.eat() // Output: Animal eats
examples.explanation: Dog class inherits from Animal, getting eat() automatically. It overrides speak() to provide its own implementation. This demonstrates inheritance and method overriding.

examples.title: Polymorphism Example
examples.description: Demonstrates runtime polymorphism using virtual functions
examples.code:
CLASS Shape
 PUBLIC:
 VIRTUAL METHOD draw()
 PRINT "Drawing shape"
 END METHOD
END CLASS

CLASS Circle EXTENDS Shape
 PUBLIC:
 METHOD draw()
 PRINT "Drawing circle"
 END METHOD
END CLASS

CLASS Rectangle EXTENDS Shape
 PUBLIC:
 METHOD draw()
 PRINT "Drawing rectangle"
 END METHOD
END CLASS

// Usage:
Shape s1 = NEW Circle()
Shape s2 = NEW Rectangle()
s1.draw() // Output: Drawing circle
s2.draw() // Output: Drawing rectangle
examples.explanation: The draw() method behaves differently based on the actual object type at runtime. This is runtime polymorphism achieved through virtual functions.

examples.title: Abstraction Example
examples.description: Shows abstract class with interface-like behavior
examples.code:
ABSTRACT CLASS Vehicle
 PUBLIC:
 ABSTRACT METHOD start()
 ABSTRACT METHOD stop()
 
 METHOD fuelCheck()
 PRINT "Checking fuel level"
 END METHOD
END CLASS

CLASS Car EXTENDS Vehicle
 PUBLIC:
 METHOD start()
 PRINT "Car engine started"
 END METHOD
 
 METHOD stop()
 PRINT "Car engine stopped"
 END METHOD
END CLASS
examples.explanation: Vehicle is an abstract class that defines the interface (what to do) without implementation. Car provides the actual implementation. Users interact with Vehicle interface without knowing implementation details.

examples.title: Method Overloading
examples.code:
CLASS Calculator
 PUBLIC:
 METHOD add(a, b)
 RETURN a + b
 END METHOD
 
 METHOD add(a, b, c)
 RETURN a + b + c
 END METHOD
 
 METHOD add(a, b) // Different types
 RETURN a + b // Concatenation if strings
 END METHOD
END CLASS

// Usage:
calc = NEW Calculator()
result1 = calc.add(5, 3) // 2 params: 8
result2 = calc.add(5, 3, 2) // 3 params: 10
examples.explanation: Multiple methods with same name but different parameters. The compiler determines which method to call based on arguments. This is compile-time (static) polymorphism.
